text: Zenyatta is a potent offense-focused Support. He stands out from the other supports due to his incredible killing power: his Orb of Destruction and Orb of Discord can let him shred through even the sturdiest of Tanks with well-aimed shots. While not as powerful a healer as Lifeweaver , his Orb of Harmony and Transcendence abilities are more than enough to let him keep a team alive when used intelligently. All of this power is counterbalanced by a severe lack of mobility. Unless Zenyatta is using his ultimate, he moves at a relatively slow pace and has no abilities to increase movement speed. This makes him vulnerable to agile flankers or large amounts of incoming fire. Weapons & Abilities [ ]   Orb of Destruction : Zenyatta's primary weapon. These fast-moving orb projectiles fire at a steady rate, dealing damage to enemies. The secondary fire is a charge ability: Zenyatta will gradually prepare up to five orbs, with the number of orbs increasing the longer he charges. He can let go of the secondary fire button at any time to release the stored orbs, firing them straight forward at his crosshairs in a rapid burst. He will automatically fire his stored orbs if the secondary fire button is held down long enough. The small size of the orbs Zenyatta fires masks their incredible damage potential. Zenyatta can out-damage many Damage heroes if he is accurate with his shots. The orbs do have a small amount of travel time. As with other projectiles, lead your shots slightly so that they will hit moving targets. Use the secondary fire to ambush stationary targets like Bastion or Widowmaker . The sudden burst of orbs can kill them instantly, not giving them time to react. These orbs have no damage falloff, meaning that they do the same amount of damage regardless of the distance to the target.   Orb of Discord : When activated, Zenyatta will place an Orb of Discord on the enemy within his crosshairs, causing them to take greatly increased damage from all sources. Orb of Discord is removed when the Zenyatta applies the Orb to a different target, loses line of sight for one and a half seconds, or dies. Orb of Discord cannot be applied to targets who are behind a barrier. This is a powerful basic ability with no cooldown between uses, though it may not be reapplied to a target within 6 seconds of that target losing the orb. Who you put this on usually depends on who the biggest threat to your team is at that moment. In general, Tanks are prime targets because they are often at the front line of the enemy team and their high health pools make them otherwise very difficult to eliminate. However, Discord can be even more valuable against enemy Damage or Support heroes that expose themselves to your team's line of fire because of the much quicker time-to-kill compared to Tanks. Try not to change the Orb's target too frequently, since that might both potentially prevent you from applying it to a target in a crucial moment and can make it more difficult for your team to follow up with focused damage. For this reason, it is often preferable to wait until an enemy is in the open to apply Discord -- otherwise they can duck behind cover for a few seconds and then have a much longer period of time where they cannot be affected by it.   Orb of Harmony : When activated, Zenyatta will place an Orb of Harmony on the targeted ally, causing them to slowly regenerate health over time. The Orb is removed when Zenyatta applies the Orb to a different target, loses line of sight for five seconds, or dies. Unlike the Orb of Discord, there is no limit to how frequently the Orb of Harmony can be applied to a single Hero. While the Orb of Harmony provides much slower healing than most other supports, the upside is that it requires little maintenance to stay activated and allows Zenyatta to focus on damaging enemies while still passively healing his team. The Orb is most effective when used proactively on teammates with low health pools, such as a flanking Genji or Tracer , since it will allow them to be more aggressive in their engagements. However, the Orb can still be used to keep tanks or other high HP alive when they are being focus fired or are disengaging due to low health. The Orb of Harmony will remain on the target for several seconds after they leave Zenyatta's line of sight. Take advantage of this by constantly ducking in and out of sight with them to refresh the duration while still avoiding enemy fire. Constantly swap the Orb between injured allies in order to keep everyone's Health topped up.   Transcendence (Ultimate): Zenyatta's Ultimate ability. When activated, he becomes invulnerable to damage for several seconds and generates an aura around himself that rapidly heals any allies standing within it. Zenyatta cannot use any other weapons or abilities while Transcendence is active, though his movement speed is increased during this time. Zenyatta does remain targetable and can still be affected by knockbacks. Transcendence should generally be used as a last resort when either Zenyatta or one of his teammates are at critical health and it is unlikely that they would otherwise survive. It can also be used proactively in situations where there is a high amount of incoming damage, at the cost of reducing the amount of outgoing damage his team is able to produce. The healing from Transcendence is massive, able to easily outpace many Ultimates like Pharah 's Barrage . Keep in mind that it will not keep an ally alive if they take a single burst of damage in excess of their maximum Health, such as from Tracer's Pulse Bomb or Junkrat's RIP-Tire. Transcendence will not heal allies if they are not in line of sight or behind an enemy barrier. Transcendence will not heal allies if they are prevented from being healed due to effects from Ana 's Biotic Grenade or Junker Queen 's Rampage. Zenyatta can still die during Transcendence if he falls off a cliff or into a pit. General Strategies [ ] Zenyatta is a challenging Support to play. With an average health pool and no way to speed himself up or dodge attacks beyond moving erratically, he has the potential to be instantly killed by a wide variety of heroes (with Hanzo and Widowmaker being the most prominent). While his abilities are rather simple, his gameplay is not, requiring you to balance helping your team by dealing damage while staying alive so that Orb of Discord and Orb of Harmony remain active. Due to Orb of Harmony's low health regen rate, it is encouraged to play Zenyatta when you have a main healer on your team (e.g. Ana , Baptiste ) or on a team that is prevalent in multiple healing abilities (e.g. Roadhog's Take A Breather, Soldier: 76 's Biotic Field, etc.). Due to Orb of Destruction's lack of damage falloff, Zenyatta can be very powerful if played in a sniper position. Do be aware though that if you are too far away, you will not be able to use Orb of Harmony or Orb of Discord. Target enemies that struggle to deal with snipers such as Tracer , Reaper , Junkrat , Bastion , and Torbjörn's turret (if you can outrange it). As above; Zenyatta's projectiles are a very effective way of dealing with an enemy Bastion in Assault configuration. Because the Orb of Destruction has no damage falloff and Bastion's minigun does, Zenyatta can consistently put a high amount of pressure onto Bastion from medium to long range whereas Bastion cannot do the same. Couple that with the Orb of Discord which negates Bastion's Assault configuration's Ironclad passive and it can make it difficult for Bastion to take an aggressive position. Try to position yourself so that you minimize the number of directions the enemy team can fire at you from. This is especially critical if the enemy team has a good Widowmaker , as a charged bodyshot will take off a large amount of your health. Keep a constant eye on nearby side routes and listen for the sounds of approaching flankers so that you can be prepared for them. Try to stay at the back of your team to minimize the amount of damage you take. Your Orb of Harmony can heal targets who are far away from you, so long as you can continue to maintain line of sight with them, and your Orb of Destruction has no damage falloff. However, if you are being constantly harassed by flankers, it can be better to stay close to your team in order to increase the risk that the flankers must take to engage you. If an enemy flanker (such as Genji or Tracer ) manages to get behind you, don't try to run; Zenyatta cannot outrun mobile heroes like them. Instead, hit them with Orb of Discord and move erratically while firing your Orb of Destruction at them. One headshot and one bodyshot from a Discord-empowered Orb of Destruction is enough to kill Tracer, assuming she doesn't have bonus Health from sources like Brigitte's Rally or Liveweaver's Tree of Life. The majority of Zenyatta's health is composed of shields. Retreating into cover for just a few seconds will activate both shield regeneration and the Support health regeneration passive, allowing him to quickly restore to full HP. The auto-targeting reticle from Orb of Discord can be useful when firing at distant enemies, as it will give you a general idea of their location. This is especially helpful if they are obscured by large particle effects (such as Hanzo's Dragonstrike ). Transcendence still allows you to be targeted by enemy abilities. Though they will deal no damage to you, they can still knock you back and away from your team, nullifying the healing on your allies. If up against enemies with strong knockbacks like Pharah or Roadhog , try to position yourself so that you will be flung into a corner or a nearby wall in order to stay near to your allies. Like Junkrat , Zenyatta can easily fire down a choke point and prevent easy movement through. His large amount of ammo and projectile weapon can provide almost constant sustained fire. If an enemy is on the other side of a corner, it's sometimes a good idea to charge a shot and release it just after turning that corner to deal a large amount of damage before they can react. If being rushed by an enemy tank with nowhere to run, you can do decent damage or even land a kill by doing the following: apply Orb of Discord, shoot twice and after the second shot (which should be your left arm) use Snap Kick to cancel the animation and maximize damage. For the best maximum damage, keep using Snap Kick against a wall to throw off the tanks aim. This isn't guaranteed to work, depending on matchup, and should only be used as a last resort.
